902:  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
903:  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
910:  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
911:  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
913:  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
915:  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
917:  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
918:  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
919:  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
924:    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
926:    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
930:  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
931:  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
932:  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
941:  buh_t   "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
943:  buh_t   "automatically:"
945:  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
947:  buh_t   "Two kludge commands, two different jobs:"
951:  buh_t   "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
952:  buh_t   "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
953:  buh_t   "so the tool does the driving for you."
958:    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
960:    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
973:  buh_c   "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
976:  buh_t   "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
977:  buh_t   "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
979:  buh_t   "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
984:  buh_t   "now you see the mechanism underneath."
989:    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" " = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
991:    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
994:    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" " = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
996:    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
1001:  buh_t   "clean tree:"
1003:  buh_c   "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
1004:  buh_c   "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
1018:  buh_t   "The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
1019:  buh_t   "network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules."
1021:  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
1023:  buh_t   "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
1024:  buh_t   "Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits"
1025:  buh_t   "for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then"
1026:  buh_t   "starts the bottle."
1031:    buh_ct  " [*] " "Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
1033:    buh_t   " [ ] Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
1042:  buh_t   "SSH into the bottle:"
1046:  buh_t   "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
1047:  buh_t   "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
1049:  buh_c   "   claude"
1051:  buh_t   "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
1052:  buh_t   "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"

content.text: There are many remaining old combinators in `first_crucible`, `payor_handbook`, and `governor_handbook`. Let me convert `first_crucible` in one large replacement. First let me read its full current state:

content.content:
860	      z_crucible_charged=1
861	    fi
862	  fi
863	
864	  # --- Header ---
865	  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
866	  buh_e
867	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
868	  buh_t   "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
869	  buh_t   "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
870	  buh_t   "beyond your own Claude subscription."
871	  buh_e
872	  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
873	  buh_t   "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
874	  buh_e
875	
876	  # Docker gate
877	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
878	    buh_E   "Docker is not available on this machine."
879	    buh_t   "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
880	    buh_e
881	    buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
882	    buh_e
883	    return 0
884	  fi
885	
886	  buh_t   "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
887	  buh_t   "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
888	  buh_e
889	
890	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
891	
892	  # =================================================================
893	  # Step 1: Build images locally
894	  # =================================================================
895	  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
896	  buh_e
897	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
898	  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
899	  buh_t   "identified by a timestamp tag."
900	  buh_e
901	  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
902	  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
903	  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
904	  buh_e
905	
906	  # --- Substep 1a: Kludge the sentry ---
907	  buh_step2 "Kludge the sentry"
908	  buh_e
909	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
910	  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
911	  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
912	  buh_e
913	  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
914	  buh_e
915	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
916	  buh_e
917	  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
918	  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
919	  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
920	  buh_e
921	
922	  # Sentry kludge image probe
923	  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
924	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
925	  else
926	    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
927	  fi
928	  buh_e
929	
930	  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
931	  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
932	  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
933	  buh_e
934	
935	  # --- Substep 1b: Kludge the bottle ---
936	  buh_step2 "Kludge the bottle"
937	  buh_e
938	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
939	  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
940	  buh_e
941	  buh_t   "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
942	  buh_line "bottle AND drives the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the nameplate"
943	  buh_t   "automatically:"
944	  buh_e
945	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
946	  buh_e
947	  buh_t   "Two kludge commands, two different jobs:"
948	  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_fk}  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
949	  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_cKB} — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
950	  buh_e
951	  buh_t   "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
952	  buh_t   "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
953	  buh_t   "so the tool does the driving for you."
954	  buh_e
955	
956	  # Bottle kludge image probe
957	  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
958	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
959	  else
960	    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
961	  fi
962	  buh_e
963	
964	  # =================================================================
965	  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
966	  # =================================================================
967	  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
968	  buh_e
969	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
970	  buh_line "what ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
971	  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
972	  buh_e
973	  buh_c   "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
974	  buh_e
975	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_RBRN} file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
976	  buh_t   "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
977	  buh_t   "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
978	  buh_e
979	  buh_t   "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
980	  buh_line "into the ${z_cmd_sentry_hallmark} field."
981	  buh_e
982	  buh_line "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what ${RBYC_HALLMARKS} are and"
983	  buh_line "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE};"
984	  buh_t   "now you see the mechanism underneath."
985	  buh_e
986	
987	  # Hallmark probes
988	  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
989	    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" " = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
990	  else
991	    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
992	  fi
993	  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
994	    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" " = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
995	  else
996	    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
997	  fi
998	  buh_e
999	
1000	  buh_line "After editing, commit the change — the next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} will need a"
1001	  buh_t   "clean tree:"
1002	  buh_e
1003	  buh_c   "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
1004	  buh_c   "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
1005	  buh_e
1006	
1007	  # =================================================================
1008	  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
1009	  # =================================================================
1010	  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
1011	  buh_e
1012	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your nameplate:"
1013	  buh_e
1014	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
1015	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
1016	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
1017	  buh_e
1018	  buh_t   "The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
1019	  buh_t   "network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules."
1020	  buh_e
1021	  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
1022	  buh_e
1023	  buh_t   "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
1024	  buh_t   "Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits"
1025	  buh_t   "for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then"
1026	  buh_t   "starts the bottle."
1027	  buh_e
1028	
1029	  # Charged probe
1030	  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
1031	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
1032	  else
1033	    buh_t   " [ ] Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
1034	  fi
1035	  buh_e
1036	
1037	  # =================================================================
1038	  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
1039	  # =================================================================
1040	  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
1041	  buh_e
1042	  buh_t   "SSH into the bottle:"
1043	  buh_e
1044	  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
1045	  buh_e
1046	  buh_t   "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
1047	  buh_t   "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
1048	  buh_e
1049	  buh_c   "   claude"
1050	  buh_e
1051	  buh_t   "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
1052	  buh_t   "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
1053	  buh_t   "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
1054	  buh_e
1055	  buh_tW  "" "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
1056	  buh_t   "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
1057	  buh_t   "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
1058	  buh_t   "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
1059	  buh_t   "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
1060	  buh_e
1061	  buh_t   "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
1062	  buh_t   "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
1063	  buh_t   "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
1064	  buh_t   "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
1065	  buh_e
1066	  buh_t   "Try your first interaction:"
1067	  buh_e
1068	  buh_c   "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
1069	  buh_e
1070	  buh_t   "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
1071	  buh_t   "The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes"
1072	  buh_t   "survive restarts."
1073	  buh_e
1074	  buh_t   "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
1075	  buh_e
1076	  buh_t   "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
1077	  buh_t   "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
1078	  buh_t   "login session with full terminal negotiation."
1079	  buh_e
1080	  buh_line "If SSH fails, ${RBYC_RACK} is the diagnostic fallback —"
1081	  buh_t   "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
1082	  buh_e
1083	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" " ${z_moniker}"
1084	  buh_e
1085	
1086	  # =================================================================
1087	  # Step 5: Verify network containment
1088	  # =================================================================
1089	  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
1090	  buh_e
1091	  buh_line "From inside the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
1092	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test"
1093	  buh_t   "target). Everything else is blocked."
1094	  buh_e
1095	  buh_t   "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
1096	  buh_e
1097	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"
1098	  buh_t   "   Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)"
1099	  buh_e
1100	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai"
1101	  buh_t   "   Expected: 403 (web app)"
1102	  buh_e
1103	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com"
1104	  buh_t   "   Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)"
1105	  buh_e
1106	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com"
1107	  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
1108	  buh_e
1109	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org"
1110	  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
1111	  buh_e
1112	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} enforces this with two layers:"
1113	  buh_t   "dnsmasq resolves only whitelisted domains; iptables drops"
1114	  buh_t   "packets to any IP not in the CIDR allowlist. Both layers"
1115	  buh_t   "must agree for traffic to pass."
1116	  buh_e
1117	  buh_line "example.com is included in the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} specifically"
1118	  buh_t   "for this verification step — it proves the allowlist works"
1119	  buh_t   "for a non-Anthropic domain."
1120	  buh_e
1121	
1122	  # =================================================================
1123	  # Step 6: Quench the crucible
1124	  # =================================================================
1125	  buh_step1 "Quench the crucible"
1126	  buh_e
1127	  buh_line "${RBYC_QUENCH} stops and removes all three containers and the"
1128	  buh_t   "enclave network:"
1129	  buh_e
1130	  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH}" "${z_moniker}"
1131	  buh_e
1132	  buh_t   "Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge"
1133	  buh_t   "reuses them instantly."
1134	  buh_e
1135	
1136	  # =================================================================
1137	  # Iteration loop
1138	  # =================================================================
1139	  buh_section "The iteration loop"
1140	  buh_e
1141	  buh_t   "You now have the full local cycle:"
1142	  buh_e
1143	  buh_t   "  1. Edit the Dockerfile or entrypoint"
1144	  buh_line "  2. Commit your changes (${RBYC_KLUDGE} needs a clean tree)"
1145	  buh_line "  3. ${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}:"
1146	  buh_tTc   "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
1147	  buh_line   "  4. Commit the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} change"
1148	  buh_line   "  5. ${RBYC_CHARGE}: Stop and restart"
1149	  buh_tI    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
1150	  buh_t     "  6. SSH in:"
1151	  buh_line  "       ${z_cmd_ssh}"
1152	  buh_t     "  7. Test your changes"
1153	  buh_e
1154	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} tears down any prior state before starting, so you"
1155	  buh_t   "don't need to Quench between iterations — just Charge again."
1156	  buh_e
1157	  buh_line "Steps 3-7 take under a minute. The ${RBYC_SENTRY} rarely changes, so"
1158	  buh_line "you almost never re-kludge it — the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your iteration"
1159	  buh_t   "target."

z_crucible_charged=1
    fi
  fi

  # --- Header ---
  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
  buh_t   "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
  buh_t   "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
  buh_t   "beyond your own Claude subscription."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
  buh_t   "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e

  # Docker gate
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
    buh_E   "Docker is not available on this machine."
    buh_t   "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
    buh_e
    buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
    buh_e
    return 0
  fi

  buh_t   "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
  buh_t   "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
  buh_e

  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  # =================================================================
  # Step 1: Build images locally
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
  buh_t   "identified by a timestamp tag."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
  buh_e

  # --- Substep 1a: Kludge the sentry ---
  buh_step2 "Kludge the sentry"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
  buh_e

  # Sentry kludge image probe
  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
  buh_e

  # --- Substep 1b: Kludge the bottle ---
  buh_step2 "Kludge the bottle"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
  buh_line "bottle AND drives the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the nameplate"
  buh_t   "automatically:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Two kludge commands, two different jobs:"
  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_fk}  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_cKB} — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
  buh_t   "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
  buh_t   "so the tool does the driving for you."
  buh_e

  # Bottle kludge image probe
  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
  buh_line "what ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_RBRN} file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
  buh_t   "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
  buh_t   "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  buh_line "into the ${z_cmd_sentry_hallmark} field."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what ${RBYC_HALLMARKS} are and"
  buh_line "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE};"
  buh_t   "now you see the mechanism underneath."
  buh_e

  # Hallmark probes
  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" " = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  fi
  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" " = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "After editing, commit the change — the next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} will need a"
  buh_t   "clean tree:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_c   "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your nameplate:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
  buh_t   "network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules."
  buh_e
  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
  buh_t   "Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits"
  buh_t   "for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then"
  buh_t   "starts the bottle."
  buh_e

  # Charged probe
  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "SSH into the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
  buh_t   "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   claude"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
  buh_t   "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
  buh_t   "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
  buh_e
  buh_tW  "" "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
  buh_t   "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
  buh_t   "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
  buh_t   "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
  buh_t   "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
  buh_t   "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
  buh_t   "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
  buh_t   "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Try your first interaction:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
  buh_t   "The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes"
  buh_t   "survive restarts."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
  buh_t   "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
  buh_t   "login session with full terminal negotiation."
  buh_e
  buh_line "If SSH fails, ${RBYC_RACK} is the diagnostic fallback —"
  buh_t   "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 5: Verify network containment
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
  buh_e
  buh_line "From inside the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test"
  buh_t   "target). Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 403 (web app)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} enforces this with two layers:"
  buh_t   "dnsmasq resolves only whitelisted domains; iptables drops"
  buh_t   "packets to any IP not in the CIDR allowlist. Both layers"
  buh_t   "must agree for traffic to pass."
  buh_e
  buh_line "example.com is included in the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} specifically"
  buh_t   "for this verification step — it proves the allowlist works"
  buh_t   "for a non-Anthropic domain."
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 6: Quench the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Quench the crucible"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_QUENCH} stops and removes all three containers and the"
  buh_t   "enclave network:"
  buh_e
  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge"
  buh_t   "reuses them instantly."
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Iteration loop
  # =================================================================
  buh_section "The iteration loop"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "You now have the full local cycle:"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "  1. Edit the Dockerfile or entrypoint"
  buh_line "  2. Commit your changes (${RBYC_KLUDGE} needs a clean tree)"
  buh_line "  3. ${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}:"
  buh_tTc   "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_line   "  4. Commit the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} change"
  buh_line   "  5. ${RBYC_CHARGE}: Stop and restart"
  buh_tI    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_t     "  6. SSH in:"
  buh_line  "       ${z_cmd_ssh}"
  buh_t     "  7. Test your changes"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} tears down any prior state before starting, so you"
  buh_t   "don't need to Quench between iterations — just Charge again."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Steps 3-7 take under a minute. The ${RBYC_SENTRY} rarely changes, so"
  buh_line "you almost never re-kludge it — the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your iteration"
  buh_t   "target."
